mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.
description: Phase 1 review fixes (empty-string normalization, case-insensitive difficulty, truncation message, named SQL columns, docs) + Phase 2 engine integration (model resolution in prompt builder, --model flag on subprocess, escalation policy injection, crash recovery escalation, IterationParams refactor, observability).

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Normalize empty-string model + case-insensitive difficulty
userStories.description: Fix two P1/P2 bugs in resolve_task_model: (1) Some('') and Some('  ') must normalize to None so they don't block difficulty escalation or PRD default fallback. (2) difficulty comparison must be case-insensitive so 'High', 'HIGH', 'hIgH' all trigger opus escalation.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_task_model guards with task_model.filter(|m| !m.trim().is_empty()) before returning
resolve_task_model(Some(''), Some('high'), None) returns Some(OPUS_MODEL), not Some('')
resolve_task_model(Some('  '), None, Some(SONNET_MODEL)) returns Some(SONNET_MODEL), not Some('  ')
difficulty comparison uses .eq_ignore_ascii_case('high') instead of == Some('high')
'High', 'HIGH', 'hIgH' all trigger opus escalation
Existing test_resolve_task_model_empty_string_is_valid_override updated to expect fall-through
Existing test_resolve_task_model_empty_string_without_fallbacks updated to expect None
Precedence table test gains 3+ new rows for empty-string and case-variant cases
Doc comment on resolve_task_model updated to note empty-string normalization and case-insensitivity
userStories.notes: These are code review findings from Phase 1. Both bugs are in the same function in model.rs. Fix both at once to avoid two passes over the same code. The empty-string guard is: `if let Some(m) = task_model { if !m.trim().is_empty() { return Some(m.to_string()); } }`. The case-insensitive check is: `if difficulty.is_some_and(|d| d.eq_ignore_ascii_case("high"))`. Update test_resolve_task_model_empty_string_is_valid_override to assert fall-through to difficulty/prd_default instead of returning Some('').

correctness: Some('') and Some('  ') must NOT short-circuit the resolution chain
Case-insensitive comparison must only trigger for 'high', not 'medium' or 'low'
Known-bad discriminator: resolve_task_model(Some(''), Some('high'), None) MUST return opus, not empty string
performance: trim().is_empty() is O(1) for typical model strings
style: Use .filter() or if-guard pattern, not nested if-let
Use .is_some_and() for difficulty check — idiomatic Rust

userStories.title: Fix truncation budget message: chars → bytes
userStories.description: The truncate_to_budget function in prompt.rs measures bytes via text.len() but the truncation message says 'chars'. Change the message to accurately say 'bytes'.
acceptanceCriteria: '[truncated to {} chars]' changed to '[truncated to {} bytes]' in truncate_to_budget
All test assertions matching the old 'chars' string updated to 'bytes'
No functional change to truncation logic itself
All existing truncation tests pass
userStories.notes: Simple string replacement. The function at prompt.rs:394 uses text.len() (byte count) and floor_char_boundary() for safe slicing. The message should accurately describe what's being measured. Find all test assertions with 'truncated to' and update them.

userStories.title: Use named columns in export load_tasks SQL
userStories.description: Change load_tasks() in export/prd.rs from positional row.get(0)? to named row.get('id')? etc. Prevents silent data corruption if columns are reordered in the SELECT.
acceptanceCriteria: All 13 row.get(N)? calls in load_tasks changed to row.get('column_name')?
load_prd_metadata if using positional access, also converted to named
All existing export tests pass without changes
Column names match the SELECT clause exactly
userStories.notes: Mechanical change. The SELECT at line 147 lists: id, title, description, priority, status, notes, acceptance_criteria, review_scope, severity, source_review, model, difficulty, escalation_note. Map each row.get(N) to the corresponding column name. Also check load_prd_metadata for the same pattern.

userStories.title: Documentation fixes for model resolution
userStories.description: Add doc comments to two non-obvious behaviors: (1) resolve_iteration_model's max_by_key tie-breaking returns the last element, (2) PrdFile.model maps to prd_metadata.default_model in the DB.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_iteration_model doc comment notes max_by_key returns last tied element
PrdFile.model field has doc comment explaining it maps to prd_metadata.default_model
No code changes, only comments
userStories.notes: Pure documentation. resolve_iteration_model is in model.rs around line 82. PrdFile is in commands/init/parse.rs. Both need a brief doc comment explaining behavior that isn't obvious from the code alone.

userStories.title: Initial tests for spawn_claude --model flag
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for the --model flag on spawn_claude(). Use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo approach to verify argument ordering. Tests define the expected new 4-parameter signature.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: model=Some('claude-opus-4-6') → echoed args contain '--model claude-opus-4-6' before '-p'
Test: model=None → echoed args do NOT contain '--model'
Test: model=Some('') → treated as None, no --model flag in args
Known-bad discriminator: test that --model appears BEFORE -p (rejects implementations that append after -p)
Test: --print and --dangerously-skip-permissions always present regardless of model
Test file compiles (tests may use #[ignore] or expected-to-fail pattern)
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo to capture args in output. spawn_claude currently has 3 params — tests define the expected 4-param signature: (prompt, signal_flag, working_dir, model: Option<&str>). Tests should verify exact arg ordering, not just presence.
edgeCases: Empty string model Some('') must be treated as None — no --model '' passed to subprocess
Model string with hyphens like 'claude-opus-4-6' — must pass through verbatim including hyphens
Very long model string — should not be truncated
invariants: --model flag must always appear BEFORE -p flag when present
When model is None, arg list must be identical to current behavior
--print and --dangerously-skip-permissions must always be present regardless of model

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test fails — flag position matters for CLI parsing
Claude CLI may error on empty model string

userStories.title: Initial tests for prompt model resolution
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for model resolution in build_prompt(). Tests verify PromptResult.resolved_model is populated correctly based on task model, difficulty, prd_default, and synergyWith relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: task with explicit model='claude-opus-4-6' → resolved_model is Some('claude-opus-4-6')
Test: task with difficulty='high' and no explicit model → resolved_model is opus
Test: task with no model, no difficulty, prd_default='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' → resolved_model is haiku
Test: task with synergyWith partner that has higher-tier model → resolved_model is the higher tier
Test: no default_model in prd_metadata → resolved_model falls back to None
Known-bad discriminator: synergy partner with opus model overrides selected task's haiku → rejects implementations that ignore synergy
Test file compiles (tests may use #[ignore] or expected-to-fail pattern)
userStories.notes: TDD: Tests define expected behavior for PromptResult.resolved_model and BuildPromptParams.default_model. Synergy cluster uses explicit synergyWith relationships from task_relationships table (NOT file overlap). Use insert_relationship() from test_utils.rs to set up synergyWith relationships. Query pattern: SELECT t.model, t.difficulty FROM tasks t INNER JOIN task_relationships tr ON tr.related_id = t.id WHERE tr.task_id = ? AND tr.rel_type = 'synergyWith' AND t.status IN ('todo', 'in_progress').
edgeCases: No default_model in prd_metadata → falls back to None
Task with empty string model Some('') → normalized to None in resolved_model
No synergyWith partners → synergy cluster is just the selected task
Multiple synergy partners with different tiers → highest tier wins
All synergy tasks are done/irrelevant → cluster is just the selected task
invariants: Tier precedence: task_model > difficulty='high' > prd_default > None
Synergy cluster model = max tier across all cluster members

failureModes.cause: prd_metadata table has no default_model column (pre-v7 DB)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Skip missing synergy partner, don't panic

Test: load_escalation_template with missing file returns None

Empty template file → Some('') returned, empty section injected (harmless)
Template file with unicode content → passed through verbatim
base_prompt_path has no parent (just 'prompt.md') → resolve relative to current dir or return None

Escalation section is NEVER present when model tier is Opus
Escalation section IS present when model tier is Default/None
Escalation section appears BEFORE reorder instruction in prompt ordering

userStories.title: Initial tests for crash recovery model escalation
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for crash recovery escalation logic. Tests verify: same-task crash triggers model escalation, different-task no escalation, None model gets sonnet baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: same task_id + last_was_crash=true → escalate_model() called, model goes up one tier
Test: different task_id + last_was_crash=true → no escalation
Test: same task_id + last_was_crash=false → no escalation
Test: escalation from haiku → sonnet, sonnet → opus verified
Test: already opus + crash → stays opus (no panic)
Test: resolved_model=None + crash → treated as SONNET_MODEL baseline, escalated to OPUS_MODEL
Known-bad discriminator: test that escalation requires BOTH same task AND crash (not just one condition)
Test file compiles (tests may use #[ignore] or expected-to-fail pattern)
userStories.notes: TDD: Tests define the escalation check logic for engine.rs. Key architect decision: crash with None model assumes SONNET_MODEL baseline and escalates to OPUS_MODEL (not a no-op). Escalation is independent of CrashTracker backoff logic — they operate separately. Test without spawning real processes.
edgeCases: First iteration: last_task_id=None → no escalation possible
Crash on task with None model → assume sonnet baseline, escalate to opus
Multiple consecutive crashes on same task → haiku→sonnet→opus→opus (ceiling)
invariants: Escalation requires BOTH same task_id AND last_was_crash
Escalation ceiling is opus — never goes beyond
None model is treated as sonnet baseline for crash escalation (architect decision)
Escalation is independent of CrashTracker backoff logic

failureModes.cause: last_task_id not set after iteration completes
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Next iteration has no crash context, no escalation

failureModes.cause: None model treated as no-op instead of sonnet baseline
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test fails — None crash must escalate to opus via sonnet baseline

userStories.title: Extract IterationParams struct from run_iteration
userStories.description: Refactor run_iteration()'s 17 positional parameters into an IterationParams struct. Purely mechanical refactor — no logic changes. This is a prerequisite for cleanly adding default_model in FEAT-007.
acceptanceCriteria: New pub struct IterationParams<'a> contains all current params except ctx: &mut IterationContext

IterationParams includes: conn, db_dir, project_root, tasks_dir, iteration, max_iterations, run_id, base_prompt_path, steering_path, inter_iteration_delay, signal_flag, elapsed_secs, verbose, usage_params, prd_path, task_prefix
run_iteration signature becomes (ctx: &mut IterationContext, params: &IterationParams) -> TaskMgrResult<IterationResult>
run_loop call site (~line 803) updated to construct IterationParams
#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] removed from run_iteration if present
Purely mechanical — no logic changes, all existing tests pass
cargo clippy clean
userStories.notes: Mechanical refactor. The compiler will catch any field mismatches. The struct definition should be placed near the top of engine.rs alongside UsageParams. All field accesses inside run_iteration change from bare names to params.field_name. The run_loop call site constructs the struct before the iteration loop. FEAT-007 will later add default_model: Option<&'a str> to this struct.

correctness: All 17 parameters correctly mapped to struct fields
No parameter reordering or type changes
Compiler catches all mismatches
performance: Zero runtime overhead — struct is passed by reference
style: Struct placed near UsageParams definition
Doc comment on struct explaining its purpose
Remove any #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]

When model is None, args unchanged: --print --dangerously-skip-permissions -p <prompt>

learnings/ingestion/mod.rs call site (~line 70) updated to pass None

All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
cargo clippy clean
userStories.notes: Guard empty strings: if let Some(m) = model { if !m.trim().is_empty() { cmd.args(['--model', m]); } }. Build args dynamically: start with ['--print', '--dangerously-skip-permissions'], optionally add ['--model', m], then add ['-p', prompt]. The args must be built BEFORE calling cmd.args() on line 58. Three call sites to update: engine.rs:349, learnings/ingestion/mod.rs:70, and existing tests that call spawn_claude directly.

correctness: --model must appear before -p in arg list
Empty string must be guarded with trim().is_empty()
Existing behavior unchanged when None
performance: No overhead when model is None
style: Use if let Some(m) pattern, not unwrap()
Guard with !m.trim().is_empty()
Build args as Vec then pass to cmd.args()

log_iteration() accepts model: Option<&str> as last parameter

Consider adding format_iteration_header() -> String for testability with print_iteration_header as thin wrapper. For progress.rs: add model param to log_iteration, add a '- Model: {}' line to the entry format. Update all 7 existing progress tests to pass None as the new last param. FEAT-007 will later change the None to the post-escalation effective_model — the model parameter receives the effective (post-crash-escalation) model, not the pre-escalation resolved_model.

userStories.title: Prompt builder model resolution
userStories.description: Wire model resolution into build_prompt(). Add default_model to BuildPromptParams, resolved_model to PromptResult. Query synergyWith tasks' model/difficulty, resolve via model::resolve_task_model and model::resolve_iteration_model. Normalize Some('') to None.
acceptanceCriteria: BuildPromptParams gains default_model: Option<&'a str> field
PromptResult gains resolved_model: Option<String> field
New fn resolve_synergy_cluster_model(conn, task_id, task_model, task_difficulty, default_model) -> Option<String>
resolve_synergy_cluster_model resolves primary task via model::resolve_task_model()
resolve_synergy_cluster_model queries pending synergyWith tasks' model/difficulty from task_relationships + tasks
resolve_synergy_cluster_model resolves each synergy task via model::resolve_task_model()
resolve_synergy_cluster_model combines all into model::resolve_iteration_model()
resolved_model is normalized: Some('') becomes None
When no synergyWith partners exist, cluster is just the selected task
When prd_metadata has no default_model, falls back to None gracefully
All existing BuildPromptParams construction sites updated with default_model: None
All TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.notes: Synergy cluster uses explicit synergyWith relationships from task_relationships table (NOT file overlap). SQL: SELECT t.model, t.difficulty FROM tasks t INNER JOIN task_relationships tr ON tr.related_id = t.id WHERE tr.task_id = ?1 AND tr.rel_type = 'synergyWith' AND t.status IN ('todo', 'in_progress'). The default_model is threaded via BuildPromptParams — not queried inside build_prompt. This was an architect decision to maintain DI and testability. Use insert_relationship() from test_utils.rs for test setup. IMPORTANT interface note: The existing resolve_iteration_model() from Phase 1 was built for file-overlap synergy clusters. This task changes the synergy source to explicit synergyWith relationships per architect decision #5. Create a new resolve_synergy_cluster_model() that collects resolved models from synergyWith partners and calls resolve_iteration_model() with pre-filtered synergy data. The PRD Public Contracts list get_synergy_task_models as a helper — the implementing agent may name this differently but must provide the same capability.

correctness: Tier precedence: task_model > difficulty='high' > prd_default > None
Synergy cluster uses highest tier across all members
Some('') normalized to None
performance: Single DB query for synergy partners' model data — no N+1
O(task_count) per iteration — acceptable for PRDs < 100 tasks
style: New helpers are pure where possible
DB queries use parameterized statements
Use existing model.rs functions — don't reimplement tier logic

userStories.title: Escalation policy template injection
userStories.description: Create escalation-policy.md template file. Add load_escalation_template() helper. In build_prompt(), inject escalation section when resolved_model tier is not Opus. Include for Default/None tier.
acceptanceCriteria: tasks/scripts/escalation-policy.md created with content from PRD appendix
New fn load_escalation_template(base_prompt_path: &Path) -> Option<String>
Template path resolved as base_prompt_path.parent()/scripts/escalation-policy.md

When resolved_model tier < Opus, escalation section injected as '## Model Escalation Policy

{contents}

---

'
When resolved_model is None (Default tier), escalation section IS injected
When resolved_model is opus, NO escalation section injected
When template file missing, returns None and prints warning to stderr
Escalation section appears BEFORE reorder instruction in prompt ordering
All TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.notes: Template is loaded fresh each iteration (not cached) to allow hot-editing. The model_tier() function from model.rs determines the tier. Inject position: after section 9 (non-code completion instruction) and before section 11 (reorder instruction) per PRD appendix ordering. Use eprintln! for missing-file warning. Key decision: include escalation for Default/None tier (CLI default could be sonnet, policy is informational). Note: PRD Public Contracts lists this as append_escalation_policy — the implementing agent may name the helper load_escalation_template or similar, either is acceptable as long as the contract (load file, check tier, inject content) is met.

correctness: Template only skipped for Opus tier, included for all others including Default/None
Section ordering matches PRD spec exactly
Missing file handled gracefully — no panic
performance: File read per iteration is acceptable — template file is small
No caching by design — enables hot-editing
style: Use std::fs::read_to_string with proper error handling
Warning to stderr, not stdout
Helper function is independently testable

This task OWNS the PrdMetadata struct change AND the read_prd_metadata() query update — do these FIRST, then wire through IterationParams, then orchestration. Crash escalation timing: AFTER build_prompt resolves base model, BEFORE spawn_claude. Import model::escalate_model and model::model_tier from loop_engine::model. For the None model crash case: effective_model = Some(OPUS_MODEL.to_string()) — don't call escalate_model(None) which returns None. userStories.title: Use named columns in engine.rs read_prd_metadata and prompt.rs get_synergy_partner_models
userStories.description: Two SQL query sites in Phase 2 code use positional row.get(N)? instead of named row.get('column_name')?, violating the pattern established by FIX-A03. Fix: (1) engine.rs read_prd_metadata row.get(0..3) → row.get('branch_name'), row.get('external_git_repo'), row.get('task_prefix'), row.get('default_model'). (2) prompt.rs get_synergy_partner_models row.get(0), row.get(1) → row.get('model'), row.get('difficulty').
acceptanceCriteria: engine.rs read_prd_metadata: row.get(0)? through row.get(3)? changed to row.get('branch_name')?, row.get('external_git_repo')?, row.get('task_prefix')?, row.get('default_model')?
prompt.rs get_synergy_partner_models: row.get(0)? changed to row.get('model')?, row.get(1)? changed to row.get('difficulty')?
Column names match the SELECT clause exactly
All existing tests pass without changes
cargo clippy clean
userStories.notes: Mechanical fix matching FIX-A03 pattern. The SELECT clauses already name the columns explicitly, so named access matches 1:1. Two sites, 6 total row.get() calls to update.

userStories.title: Review implementation for refactoring opportunities

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for prompt model resolution

acceptanceCriteria: Test: multiple synergy partners with mixed tiers (haiku + opus) → opus wins

Test: all synergy partners have None model → falls back to prd_default
Test: empty synergyWith list → cluster is just selected task
Test: difficulty='high' with no task model and no prd_default → resolved is opus
80%+ coverage for model resolution path in build_prompt
userStories.notes: Added 3 comprehensive tests: synergy partner with None model + prd_default, medium difficulty falls to prd_default, all synergy None falls to prd_default. Total 13 model resolution tests covering all tier precedence combinations and synergy topologies.

Synergy cluster resolution correct for all topology shapes

Test: crash escalation is independent of CrashTracker backoff count
Test: None model crash → opus (via sonnet baseline assumption)
userStories.notes: Added 3 comprehensive tests: success resets escalation, task boundary isolation, independence from CrashTracker. Total 13 crash escalation tests covering all escalation paths and edge cases.

correctness: Multi-crash escalation ladder is correct

userStories.title: Integration test: end-to-end model resolution through subprocess
userStories.description: End-to-end integration test verifying the full model selection pipeline: PRD with model fields → DB → build_prompt → resolved_model → spawn_claude with --model flag. Use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo to verify args.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: PRD with default_model=haiku + one high-difficulty task → iteration uses opus (--model claude-opus-4-6 in args)
Test: PRD with default_model=sonnet + task with explicit opus model → iteration uses opus
Test: PRD with no default_model + task with no model → no --model flag in args
Test: escalation template appears in prompt for haiku/sonnet iterations but not opus
Test: iteration header displays the correct model name
Test: PRD with mixed task models (haiku default, one sonnet override, one high-difficulty) → each iteration selects the correct model
Test: progress.txt model field contains the correct model name after iteration
All tests use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo and temp DB for isolation
userStories.notes: These tests exercise the full pipeline end-to-end. Import/init a test PRD with model fields, run one or more iterations with CLAUDE_BINARY=echo, and verify the model flows correctly through to the subprocess args and prompt content. Reference existing integration tests in tests/ directory for patterns. Covers US-P2-006 from Phase 1 PRD: end-to-end integration test with mixed models.

correctness: Full pipeline verified: PRD → DB → prompt → subprocess args
Escalation template presence correlates with model tier
performance: Tests must complete in <30 seconds each
style: Use test fixtures consistent with existing integration test patterns
